title: Step 2: Define Target Scenarios
subtitle: Configure one or more target server specs and sizing assumptions.

scenarioCard.seededNotice: Seeded from your import: {{ratio}}:1 ratio, {{growth}}% growth, {{safety}}% safety — adjust as needed.

serverConfig.heading: Target Server Config
serverConfig.noSocketCoreData: No socket/core data from import — enter manually.

tooltips.socketsPerServer: Physical CPU sockets per target server. Check the vendor spec sheet.
tooltips.coresPerSocket: Physical cores per socket — NOT hyperthreaded logical CPUs.
tooltips.ramPerServerGb: Total RAM installed per target server (GB).
tooltips.diskPerServerGb: Total usable storage per target server (GB).
tooltips.targetVcpuToPCoreRatio: Hard assignment-density cap: no more than N vCPUs per physical core. VMware recommends 4:1 for mixed workloads; use 2:1 for databases.
tooltips.ramPerVmGb: Average RAM per VM in your current cluster. In vCenter: Monitor → Memory Used ÷ VM count. Available from LiveOptics/RVTools import.
tooltips.diskPerVmGb: Average provisioned disk per VM. Auto-filled from Total Disk GB ÷ Total VMs when available. Or read from RVTools/LiveOptics import.
tooltips.growthPercent: Future workload growth. 0% = size for today only. Scales all demand (CPU, RAM, disk).
tooltips.safetyPercent: Operational buffer so the cluster never runs hot. 20% → sized to run ~83% under current load.
tooltips.targetSpecint: SPECrate2017_int_base score for the target server. Find at spec.org/cpu2017/results/ → filter by the new server model. Default is Dell R660 with 2× Xeon Gold 6526Y (337).
tooltips.targetCpuFrequencyGhz: Target server CPU clock frequency in GHz. Used with current frequency to compute GHz demand ratio.
tooltips.minServerCount: Pin a minimum floor: the final server count will never go below this value, regardless of the computed sizing.

results.noData: Enter cluster data in Step 1 to see results.
